name: Supplier ESG Assessment Framework
description: Standardised framework for assessing supplier ESG performance including questionnaire templates, scoring methodology, tier classification, and corrective action plans

labels.1: Non-compliant — No evidence of policies, systems, or practices
labels.2: Initial — Some awareness but no formal systems; reactive approach
labels.3: Developing — Formal policies exist but implementation is inconsistent or incomplete
labels.4: Established — Systematic approach with documented processes, regular monitoring, and evidence of effectiveness
labels.5: Leading — Best-in-class practices, continuous improvement, third-party verified, industry leadership

riskClassification.label: Critical Risk
riskClassification.action: Immediate corrective action required; consider relationship suspension

riskClassification.label: High Risk
riskClassification.action: Corrective action plan within 30 days; enhanced monitoring

riskClassification.label: Medium Risk
riskClassification.action: Improvement plan within 90 days; regular monitoring

questionnaireCategories.name: Environmental Management
questionnaireCategories.description: Assessment of supplier's environmental management systems, resource efficiency, emissions, and environmental compliance

questions.question: Does the supplier have a certified Environmental Management System (e.g., ISO 14001)?

scoringGuidance.1: No environmental policy or management system
scoringGuidance.2: Informal environmental practices, no documented policy
scoringGuidance.3: Environmental policy exists; management system in development
scoringGuidance.4: Certified EMS (ISO 14001 or equivalent) implemented and maintained
scoringGuidance.5: Certified EMS with regular third-party audits, continuous improvement programme, and public reporting

questions.question: Does the supplier measure and report greenhouse gas emissions (Scope 1, 2, and relevant Scope 3)?

scoringGuidance.2: Awareness of GHG emissions but no systematic measurement
scoringGuidance.3: Scope 1 and 2 measured; Scope 3 not yet addressed
scoringGuidance.4: Scope 1, 2, and material Scope 3 measured and reported; reduction targets set
scoringGuidance.5: Comprehensive GHG inventory (all scopes), science-based targets, third-party verified, annual disclosure

questions.question: Does the supplier have water management practices and track water consumption?

scoringGuidance.2: Basic awareness, no tracking
scoringGuidance.3: Water consumption tracked; some conservation measures
scoringGuidance.4: Comprehensive water management plan, reduction targets, wastewater treatment
scoringGuidance.5: Water stewardship programme, water risk assessment, zero liquid discharge or water-positive targets

questions.question: Does the supplier have a waste management programme including reduction, recycling, and proper disposal?

scoringGuidance.2: Basic waste disposal but no tracking or reduction goals
scoringGuidance.3: Waste tracked; some recycling and reduction efforts
scoringGuidance.4: Comprehensive waste management, diversion targets, hazardous waste properly handled
scoringGuidance.5: Zero waste to landfill programme, circular economy practices, third-party audited

questions.question: Does the supplier have processes to identify and manage environmental risks in its own supply chain?

scoringGuidance.1: No consideration of supply chain environmental risks
scoringGuidance.2: Awareness but no formal processes
scoringGuidance.3: Environmental criteria included in some procurement decisions
scoringGuidance.4: Systematic environmental screening of suppliers, contractual environmental requirements
scoringGuidance.5: Full supply chain environmental risk mapping, cascade of requirements, joint improvement programmes

questions.question: Does the supplier comply with all applicable environmental laws and regulations, with no outstanding violations?

scoringGuidance.2: Recent violations, corrective action underway
scoringGuidance.3: Compliant with applicable laws; minor historical non-conformances resolved
scoringGuidance.4: Full compliance, proactive regulatory engagement, internal compliance monitoring
scoringGuidance.5: Exceeds regulatory requirements, participates in voluntary environmental initiatives

questionnaireCategories.name: Labour Practices
questionnaireCategories.description: Assessment of supplier's labour standards, worker rights, fair wages, working hours, and freedom of association

questions.question: Does the supplier have policies prohibiting child labour and forced labour, aligned with ILO core conventions?

scoringGuidance.1: No policies on child or forced labour
scoringGuidance.2: Informal prohibition but no documented policy or monitoring
scoringGuidance.3: Written policy exists; basic age verification and employment contract practices
scoringGuidance.4: Comprehensive policy aligned with ILO C138, C182, C29, C105; systematic monitoring and remediation
scoringGuidance.5: ILO-aligned policy, third-party audited, supply chain cascade, participation in industry initiatives (e.g., Fair Labor Association)

questions.question: Does the supplier ensure fair wages (at or above legal minimum) and reasonable working hours for all workers?

scoringGuidance.1: Evidence of below-minimum wages or excessive mandatory overtime
scoringGuidance.2: Minimum wage compliance but excessive working hours or unpaid overtime
scoringGuidance.3: Legal minimum wages paid; working hours within legal limits; overtime compensated
scoringGuidance.4: Wages above local minimum; living wage analysis conducted; working hours well within limits
scoringGuidance.5: Living wage paid; working hours optimised; worker satisfaction regularly assessed

questions.question: Does the supplier respect freedom of association and the right to collective bargaining?

scoringGuidance.2: No explicit policy; worker committees or unions not actively supported
scoringGuidance.3: Written policy respecting freedom of association; no active interference
scoringGuidance.4: Active engagement with worker representatives; collective bargaining supported where applicable
scoringGuidance.5: Formal collective bargaining agreements in place; worker representatives involved in decision-making

questions.question: Does the supplier have a non-discrimination and equal opportunity policy covering hiring, promotion, and compensation?

scoringGuidance.2: Informal practices; no documented policy
scoringGuidance.3: Written non-discrimination policy; basic training provided
scoringGuidance.4: Comprehensive policy covering all protected characteristics; diversity monitoring; grievance mechanism
scoringGuidance.5: Diversity and inclusion programme, regular pay equity analysis, third-party benchmarked

questions.question: Does the supplier ensure ethical recruitment practices (no recruitment fees, no document retention)?

scoringGuidance.1: Workers charged recruitment fees or documents confiscated
scoringGuidance.2: No clear policy on recruitment practices
scoringGuidance.3: Policy prohibiting recruitment fees; workers retain documents
scoringGuidance.4: Employer Pays Principle adopted; recruitment agencies audited; migrant worker protections
scoringGuidance.5: IRIS Standard or equivalent ethical recruitment certification; independent verification of recruitment channels

questions.question: Does the supplier provide accessible and effective grievance mechanisms for workers?

scoringGuidance.2: Informal complaint process only
scoringGuidance.3: Formal grievance mechanism exists; available to all workers
scoringGuidance.4: Confidential, accessible, culturally appropriate mechanism; non-retaliation policy; timely resolution
scoringGuidance.5: Multi-channel grievance system (hotline, digital, in-person); independent oversight; worker trust verified through surveys

questions.question: Does the supplier ensure equal treatment and protections for migrant, temporary, and contract workers?

scoringGuidance.1: Migrant/temporary workers exploited or excluded from protections
scoringGuidance.2: Some protections but gaps in coverage
scoringGuidance.3: Equal treatment policy exists for all worker categories
scoringGuidance.4: Comprehensive protections, regular monitoring of conditions for all worker types
scoringGuidance.5: All workers, regardless of status, receive equal benefits, protections, and grievance access; independently verified

questionnaireCategories.name: Health and Safety
questionnaireCategories.description: Assessment of supplier's occupational health and safety management, incident tracking, and workplace conditions

questions.question: Does the supplier have a certified Occupational Health and Safety Management System (e.g., ISO 45001)?

scoringGuidance.2: Basic safety practices but no formal system
scoringGuidance.3: H&S management system in place; working toward certification
scoringGuidance.4: Certified OHS management system (ISO 45001 or equivalent)
scoringGuidance.5: Certified OHS, zero-harm culture, industry-leading safety performance, continuous improvement

questions.question: Does the supplier track and report workplace incidents (injuries, illnesses, fatalities, near-misses)?

scoringGuidance.2: Major incidents recorded but no systematic tracking
scoringGuidance.3: Incidents tracked; LTIFR calculated; reported to management
scoringGuidance.4: Comprehensive incident tracking including near-misses; root cause analysis; trend reporting
scoringGuidance.5: Real-time incident reporting, predictive safety analytics, benchmarked performance, public disclosure

questions.question: Does the supplier provide adequate personal protective equipment (PPE) and safety training to all workers?

scoringGuidance.1: No PPE provided or training conducted
scoringGuidance.2: Some PPE available but not consistently used; minimal training
scoringGuidance.3: Appropriate PPE provided; regular safety training conducted
scoringGuidance.4: Comprehensive PPE programme, role-specific training, competency verification
scoringGuidance.5: Advanced safety training including behavioural safety; PPE innovation; worker safety champions

questions.question: Does the supplier have emergency preparedness plans (fire safety, evacuation, first aid, chemical spill response)?

scoringGuidance.2: Basic fire extinguishers present; no formal plans
scoringGuidance.3: Emergency plans documented; annual evacuation drills
scoringGuidance.4: Comprehensive emergency preparedness; regular drills; trained first responders
scoringGuidance.5: Advanced emergency preparedness, business continuity integration, community coordination

questions.question: Does the supplier manage hazardous materials and chemical safety in accordance with applicable regulations?

scoringGuidance.2: Some hazardous materials identified but no systematic management
scoringGuidance.3: Chemical inventory maintained; SDS available; basic handling procedures
scoringGuidance.4: Comprehensive chemical management programme; REACH/GHS compliance; exposure monitoring
scoringGuidance.5: Substitution programme for hazardous chemicals; advanced exposure controls; third-party verified

questionnaireCategories.name: Ethics and Governance
questionnaireCategories.description: Assessment of supplier's business ethics, anti-corruption, data privacy, and governance practices

questions.question: Does the supplier have a code of ethics/conduct covering anti-bribery, anti-corruption, and conflicts of interest?

scoringGuidance.2: Informal expectations; no documented code
scoringGuidance.3: Written code of conduct; distributed to employees
scoringGuidance.4: Comprehensive code, regular training, whistleblower channel, compliance investigations
scoringGuidance.5: Third-party anti-corruption audits; certified compliance programme; public disclosure

questions.question: Does the supplier have a confidential whistleblower/reporting mechanism for ethics violations?

scoringGuidance.2: Informal reporting only (e.g., direct to manager)
scoringGuidance.3: Formal channel exists; basic non-retaliation policy
scoringGuidance.4: Confidential, multi-channel reporting system; independent investigation; non-retaliation enforced
scoringGuidance.5: Third-party managed hotline; anonymous reporting; board-level oversight of reported incidents

questions.question: Does the supplier comply with applicable data privacy and protection laws (e.g., GDPR, CCPA)?

scoringGuidance.2: Basic awareness but no formal programme
scoringGuidance.3: Privacy policy exists; basic compliance measures
scoringGuidance.4: Comprehensive data protection programme; DPO appointed; privacy impact assessments
scoringGuidance.5: Privacy by design; certified (e.g., ISO 27701); regular third-party audits

questions.question: Does the supplier have transparent tax practices and not engage in aggressive tax avoidance?

scoringGuidance.1: Evidence of tax evasion or fraudulent practices
scoringGuidance.2: No tax transparency; aggressive structures
scoringGuidance.3: Compliant with local tax laws; no public reporting
scoringGuidance.4: Tax transparency policy; country-by-country reporting considered
scoringGuidance.5: Public tax strategy; GRI 207 alignment; participates in responsible tax initiatives

questions.question: Does the supplier have effective governance structures with clear accountability for ESG matters?

scoringGuidance.2: ESG responsibility not formally assigned
scoringGuidance.3: ESG responsibility assigned to specific function/individual
scoringGuidance.4: Board-level ESG oversight; ESG KPIs in management performance evaluation
scoringGuidance.5: Dedicated ESG committee; ESG integrated into strategy and risk management; external advisory

questionnaireCategories.name: Supply Chain Management
questionnaireCategories.description: Assessment of supplier's own supply chain due diligence, sub-supplier management, and traceability

questions.question: Does the supplier have a supplier code of conduct that cascades ESG requirements to its own suppliers?

scoringGuidance.2: Informal expectations of sub-suppliers
scoringGuidance.3: Supplier code of conduct exists; communicated to key suppliers
scoringGuidance.4: Code incorporated into contracts; compliance monitored; corrective action process
scoringGuidance.5: Code cascaded to all tiers; regular audits; collaborative improvement programmes

questions.question: Does the supplier conduct ESG risk assessments of its own supply chain?

scoringGuidance.1: No supply chain ESG risk assessment
scoringGuidance.2: Awareness of risks but no systematic assessment
scoringGuidance.3: Risk assessment of critical Tier 1 suppliers
scoringGuidance.4: Systematic risk assessment of Tier 1 and key Tier 2 suppliers; risk prioritisation
scoringGuidance.5: Comprehensive multi-tier risk mapping; dynamic risk monitoring; industry collaboration

questions.question: Does the supplier audit or assess its sub-suppliers for ESG compliance?

scoringGuidance.2: Self-assessment questionnaires for some suppliers
scoringGuidance.3: Questionnaire-based assessment of key suppliers; some on-site audits
scoringGuidance.4: Regular audits of critical suppliers; third-party audits for high-risk suppliers
scoringGuidance.5: Comprehensive audit programme; unannounced audits; beyond-audit approaches (worker voice)

questions.question: Does the supplier have supply chain traceability systems for critical raw materials or components?

scoringGuidance.2: Tier 1 supplier information only
scoringGuidance.3: Traceability to Tier 2 for critical materials
scoringGuidance.4: Traceability to raw material origin for key risk materials; chain of custody documentation
scoringGuidance.5: Full supply chain traceability using technology (blockchain, digital passports); third-party verified

questions.question: Does the supplier have responsible sourcing policies for conflict minerals (3TG) and other high-risk materials?

scoringGuidance.2: Awareness but no formal policy
scoringGuidance.3: Conflict minerals policy exists; CMRT collected from key suppliers
scoringGuidance.4: Comprehensive responsible sourcing policy; smelter-level traceability; conformant sources prioritised
scoringGuidance.5: Full alignment with OECD Due Diligence Guidance; RMI participation; public smelter list disclosure

questions.question: Does the supplier have a process for managing non-conformances and corrective actions with its sub-suppliers?

scoringGuidance.2: Ad-hoc response to issues
scoringGuidance.3: Formal corrective action process; timelines for remediation
scoringGuidance.4: Systematic non-conformance management; root cause analysis; follow-up verification
scoringGuidance.5: Comprehensive CAP system with escalation protocol; capacity building support; disengagement as last resort

riskScoringMethodology.description: Composite risk score combining country risk, sector risk, and company-level risk

countryRisk.description: Risk associated with country of operations based on governance, rule of law, labour protections

sectorRisk.description: Risk associated with the industry sector based on known ESG risks

companyRisk.description: Company-specific risk derived from the questionnaire assessment score (inverted: lower assessment = higher risk)
companyRisk.derivation: companyRisk = 6 - assessmentScore (e.g., assessment score of 4 = company risk of 2)

tierClassification.description: Supplier tier classification determines the level of due diligence assessment required

tiers.name: Direct / Critical Suppliers
criteria: Top suppliers by annual spend (e.g., top 80% of total procurement spend)
Suppliers of critical components or raw materials
Suppliers in high-risk countries or sectors
tiers.assessmentDescription: Full ESG assessment: complete questionnaire, on-site audit, documentation review, worker interviews
tiers.frequency: Annual assessment; continuous monitoring for highest-risk suppliers
tiers.escalation: Non-conformances trigger corrective action plan with defined timelines

tiers.name: Significant Suppliers
criteria: Moderate spend suppliers (e.g., next 15% of procurement spend)
Suppliers of important but not critical materials or services
Suppliers in medium-risk countries or sectors
tiers.assessmentDescription: Questionnaire-based assessment with desktop review; targeted on-site audits for elevated-risk suppliers
tiers.frequency: Assessment every 2 years; more frequently if risk profile changes
tiers.escalation: Significant non-conformances trigger upgrade to Tier 1 assessment

tiers.name: Low-Spend / Low-Risk Suppliers
criteria: Low annual spend (e.g., remaining 5% of procurement spend)
Suppliers of non-critical goods or services
Suppliers in low-risk countries and sectors
tiers.assessmentDescription: Screening-only: sanctions/watchlist check, basic compliance verification, self-declaration
tiers.frequency: Every 3 years or upon trigger event
tiers.escalation: Adverse media or compliance flags trigger upgrade to Tier 2

correctiveActionPlanTemplate.description: Template for documenting and tracking corrective actions from supplier assessments

fields.description: Unique identifier for the corrective action
Description of the non-conformance or finding identified during assessment

descriptions.critical: Immediate threat to life, forced/child labour, severe environmental damage — requires immediate action
descriptions.major: Systematic failure of management systems, significant legal non-compliance — requires action within 30 days
descriptions.minor: Isolated non-conformance, limited impact — requires action within 90 days
descriptions.observation: Opportunity for improvement, not a non-conformance — for consideration

fields.description: Root cause analysis of the finding
Specific corrective action to be taken

properties.targetDate: Date by which corrective action must be completed

properties.supplierContact: Name and role of person responsible at the supplier
properties.buyerContact: Name and role of person responsible at the buyer

properties.requiredEvidence: Description of evidence needed to close the finding (e.g., updated policy document, audit report, photographic evidence, worker interviews)
properties.verificationMethod: How closure will be verified (e.g., desktop review, on-site verification, third-party audit)
properties.closureDate: Date the corrective action was verified as closed

fields.description: Current status of the corrective action
